questions.question: What is a polynomial?
questions.correct_answer: An algebraic expression with non-negative integer powers of variables
questions.explanation: Polynomials contain variables raised only to whole number powers.

questions.question: What is the degree of a polynomial?
questions.correct_answer: The highest power of the variable in the polynomial
questions.explanation: Degree is determined by the maximum exponent of the variable.

questions.question: What is a linear polynomial?
questions.explanation: Linear polynomials are of the form ax + b.

questions.question: What is a quadratic polynomial?
questions.explanation: Quadratic polynomials are of the form ax² + bx + c.

questions.question: What is the zero of a polynomial?
questions.correct_answer: A value of the variable for which the polynomial becomes zero
questions.explanation: Zeroes are found by solving p(x) = 0.

questions.question: How are zeroes of a polynomial related to its graph?
questions.correct_answer: Zeroes correspond to the x-intercepts of the graph
questions.explanation: Where the graph cuts the x-axis, the polynomial value is zero.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of zeroes of a polynomial?
questions.correct_answer: Equal to the degree of the polynomial
questions.explanation: A polynomial of degree n can have at most n zeroes.

questions.question: What is the degree of the zero polynomial?
questions.correct_answer: The degree of the zero polynomial is not defined
questions.explanation: Zero polynomial has no non-zero term.

questions.question: Which polynomial represents a straight line graph?
questions.explanation: Graphs of linear polynomials are straight lines.

notes.title: Forms of Polynomials
notes.content: Linear, quadratic and cubic polynomials are classified based on degree as 1, 2 and 3 respectively.

notes.title: Zeroes and Graphs
notes.content: The number of zeroes of a polynomial is equal to the number of times its graph cuts the x-axis.

notes.title: Degree Concept
notes.content: The degree of a polynomial helps in predicting the maximum number of zeroes.

notes.title: Constant Polynomial
notes.content: A constant polynomial has degree zero and its graph is a horizontal line.

notes.title: Zero Polynomial
notes.content: The zero polynomial has undefined degree and its value is always zero.

facts.fact: A polynomial of degree one always represents a straight line.
A quadratic polynomial can have at most two zeroes.
Zeroes of a polynomial are also called roots.
Graphs help in visualising the nature and number of zeroes.
